name: Rehearsal and mnemonic strategies
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to memorize material by overt or covert practice.
student_phrases: Repeat important facts or formulas many times
methods: Repeat important facts or formulas many times
positive_indicators: I write it until I remember

name: Practice testing
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to practice recalling the material by working through questions.
student_phrases: Make your own questions and answer them
Try to remember material without looking
methods: Make your own questions and answer them
Try to remember material without looking

name: Repetition over time
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to review material studied in earlier sessions at later times.
student_phrases: Study the same material again after a few days
Spread study time out instead of cramming
Set reminders to review old topics
methods: Study the same material again after a few days
Spread study time out instead of cramming
Set reminders to review old topics

name: Reviewing previously compiled records
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to reread tests, notes, or textbooks to prepare for class or further testing.
student_phrases: Look over old diagrams or charts
methods: Look over old diagrams or charts

name: Highlighting and underlining
category: Selecting, organizing, and transforming key pieces of information
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to mark the central points of material to be learned.
student_phrases: Highlight only the most important words
Use different colors for different themes
Add short notes next to highlighted text
Mark confusing parts to revisit later
methods: Highlight only the most important words
Use different colors for different themes
Add short notes next to highlighted text
Mark confusing parts to revisit later

name: Organizing and transforming
category: Selecting, organizing, and transforming key pieces of information
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated overt or covert rearrangement of instructional materials to improve learning.
student_phrases: Turn long text into bullet points
Rewrite content in your own structure (tables, lists)
methods: Turn long text into bullet points
Rewrite content in your own structure (tables, lists)

name: Keeping records and monitoring
category: Selecting, organizing, and transforming key pieces of information
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to record events or results.
student_phrases: Keep a list of mistakes to fix
Track vocabulary or formulas you miss
Check off what you have completed
methods: Keep a list of mistakes to fix
Track vocabulary or formulas you miss
Check off what you have completed

name: Visualization
category: Selecting, organizing, and transforming key pieces of information
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to create explanative or organizational drawings to illustrate lesson contents or forming mental images of the material.
student_phrases: Imagine the information in your mind
methods: Imagine the information in your mind

name: Teaching and preparing to teach
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to understand material by explaining it to someone or preparing to do so.
student_phrases: Explain the topic to a friend
Teach someone younger or less experienced
Make a short presentation for practice
methods: Explain the topic to a friend
Teach someone younger or less experienced
Make a short presentation for practice

name: Self-Explanation
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to understand material by verbally or mentally explaining how new information is related to previously learned material.
student_phrases: Explain each step as you solve a problem
Talk through how new ideas connect to old ones
Write down short explanations in your own words
methods: Explain each step as you solve a problem
Talk through how new ideas connect to old ones
Write down short explanations in your own words

name: Goal setting and planning
definitions: Statements indicating student setting of educational goals or subgoals and planning for prioritizing, sequencing, timing, and completing activities related to those goals.
student_phrases: Break big tasks into small steps
Plan study time on a calendar
methods: Break big tasks into small steps
Plan study time on a calendar

name: Self-evaluating and regulation
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated evaluations of the quality or progress of their work and their own level of understanding.
student_phrases: Ask yourself if you really understand
Look for what you still don’t get
Change strategies if something isn’t working
Use rubrics or checklists to track progress
methods: Ask yourself if you really understand
Look for what you still don’t get
Change strategies if something isn’t working
Use rubrics or checklists to track progress
positive_indicators: I check if I did it right

name: Environmental structuring
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to select or arrange the physical setting to make learning easier.
student_phrases: Choose a place with good light and no distractions
methods: Choose a place with good light and no distractions

name: Self-consequences
definitions: Statements indicating student arrangement or imagination of rewards or punishment for success or failure.
student_phrases: Reward yourself after finishing a task
Set small treats after study sessions
Use “If I do X, then I can do Y” rules
Limit fun activities until study goals are done
Track your rewards to stay motivated
methods: Reward yourself after finishing a task
Set small treats after study sessions
Use “If I do X, then I can do Y” rules
Limit fun activities until study goals are done
Track your rewards to stay motivated

name: Seeking social assistance
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to solicit help from fellow students.
student_phrases: Message a friend about confusing topics
methods: Message a friend about confusing topics

name: Seeking and selecting information
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to secure further information from nonsocial sources when undertaking a task.
student_phrases: Read additional articles or textbook pages
methods: Read additional articles or textbook pages

name: Time and attention management
definitions: Statements indicating student-initiated efforts to structure their work periods and break times taking into account other commitments, motivation and focus.
student_phrases: Plan your most difficult tasks for your “best”
methods: Plan your most difficult tasks for your “best”
Start with a short study session to “warm up”

name: Other / Externally Regulated
definitions: Statements indicating learning behavior that is initiated by other persons such as teachers or parents, and all unclear verbal responses.
